retry_reason: Failure (NULL return) indicates the requested root does not exist in this filesystem instance; its existence is determined at mount time and does not change during normal operation, so retrying will not help.
contract_summary: Acquire (get) a reference to a global btrfs_root identified by objectid. The caller must check the return value: if non-NULL, the caller owns a reference and must release it with btrfs_put_root() when done. If NULL, the requested root is not available (e.g., not initialized). The function does not create roots; it only increments the refcount of an existing root. The mapping from objectid to root pointer is static per filesystem instance. No locks or RCU are required beyond what btrfs_grab_root internally uses.
